title: Probe-count divergence WARNING fires false-positive on every phase12_deploy CLI path
summary: The silent-failure-hunter MED conf-8 fix introduced an `if n != input_total: WARNING` check. When phase12_deploy.py passes cli_p999/cli_count from a SQL probe run minutes earlier, `n = cli_count` is a stale snapshot — comparing it to a fresh PostgREST `len(rows.data)` will diverge whenever an analytics_runner inserts/deletes a strategy row between the two reads (legitimate temporal drift, not a role/RLS asymmetry). Without this fix the WARNING would fire on every legitimate deploy.
evidence: phase12_kill_switch.py main(): cli_p999/cli_count bypass the in-process probe at measure_p999; the comparison only makes sense when both `n` and `input_total` came from the same Python process within ms of each other.
fix: Gate the check on `probe_was_in_process = cli_p999 is None or cli_count is None` so the divergence WARNING only fires when both counts came from the same process. APPLIED.

title: _atomic_append_todos still leaks tempfile on write/fsync failure (code-reviewer LOW conf-9 NOT applied)
summary: The code-reviewer LOW conf-9 finding was skipped by the specialist apply pass but meets the LOW≥9 gate threshold. NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) creates the file with a unique name; if tmp.write(payload) or os.fsync(tmp.fileno()) raises (disk full, EIO), the with-block exits via exception, tmp_path is leaked on disk, and the function raises without cleanup. Audit directory accumulates orphan .phase12_kill_switch_audit_*.tmp files.
evidence: phase12_kill_switch.py:690-712 the prior shape wrapped the writes in a context manager whose cleanup only fired on os.replace failure.
fix: Restructure to capture tmp_path BEFORE writes, wrap write/fsync/replace in try/except BaseException, unlink on any failure. APPLIED.

title: Hardcoded PGPASSFILE/PGSERVICEFILE nulls could be silently overridden if _parse_postgres_url is extended
summary: `subprocess_env = {**clean_env, 'PGPASSFILE': '', 'PGSERVICEFILE': '', **pg_env}` — pg_env overrides at the end. If a future maintainer extends _parse_postgres_url to derive PGPASSFILE from a DSN query param (e.g. ?passfile=/var/secrets/pgpass), the null would be silently overridden. The current behavior is correct because _parse_postgres_url only emits PGHOST/PGPORT/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD/PGDATABASE/PGSSLMODE, but the manual hardcoded null lines couple subprocess_env to _parse_postgres_url's output shape.
evidence: phase12_kill_switch.py:394 — comment notes the null is to defeat libpq fallback resolution but doesn't note the implicit dependency on pg_env's output shape.
fix: Document the implicit invariant in the comment so a future extension of _parse_postgres_url surfaces the consideration. SKIPPED — adds comment noise without behavior change; the existing ALSO-stripped clean_env path is the primary defense.

title: AUDIT stderr line uses ' | ' separator but TODOS.md write still gets the multi-line form (intentional asymmetry; document)
summary: The code-reviewer LOW conf-9 fix replaces internal newlines with ' | ' in the stderr emit but keeps the markdown multi-line form for the file write. This is the correct asymmetry (markdown wants newlines; log aggregators want single-line), but the dual contract isn't documented.
fix: Already covered by the inline comment; no additional fix needed. NOT APPLIED.
